subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables, graphs and sample calculations are clearly laid out and labelled, with units and uncertainties usually indicated.
Sample calculation of Q-factor is well demonstrated, aiding reproducibility of the processing method.
Numerical processing is internally consistent and free from major arithmetic errors.
weaknesses: Uncertainties are estimated by a simplistic half-range method, and propagation into the final Q-factor analysis is not shown.
Figures and graphs lack complete labels (axes, units) and error bars or statistical metrics (e.g., RÂ²) are missing.
Outliers in the raw data table are unaddressed, and there is no discussion of instrument resolution or goodness-of-fit.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data recording and processing is clear and generally precise (band 4), and processing itself is accurate (band 4).  Although the treatment of uncertainties has significant omissions (band 3), the overall quality of the numerical work and clarity justifies placing this criterion at the top end of the 3â4 band, moving into the 5â6 band on balance, for a final score of 5.

strengths: The research question is precisely framed within a specific and appropriate physical context, detailing the springâmass system and disc diameters.
The description of methodologyâincluding variables, apparatus, and a step-by-step procedureâallows an investigator to reproduce the core of the experiment.
Controlled-variables table demonstrates awareness of relevant factors and includes justification for mass compensation of the 20 cm disc.
weaknesses: Important operational details are omitted (e.g., initial displacement size, sensor sampling rate and calibration, method for identifying successive maxima).
The Q-factor formula is presented without derivation steps or citation, and symbols ($x_i$, $x_f$) are undefined in the text.
Some background material is overly narrative; the theoretical discussion could be more concise and directly focused on the role of cardboard discs in damping.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate a very strong contextualization of the research question (band 5), a solid procedural description (band 4) and a described (but not fully explained) account of methodological considerations (band 3).  On balance, the work sits comfortably at the top of the 3â4 band heading into the 5â6 band for contextualization, warranting a score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: The conclusion succinctly justifies the main finding and is fully consistent with the analyzed data and original hypothesis.
Integration of peer-reviewed literature situates the result within an accepted scientific context.
Logical linkage between data trends, the quantitative Q-factor results, and theoretical framework is clear.
weaknesses: Statistical strength (e.g., goodness-of-fit, confidence intervals) is not discussed.
The comparison to published values is qualitative and lacks numerical agreement or deeper critical analysis.
The discussion of broader implications is brief and could be expanded with additional context.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is well supported by the analysis and clearly justified (band 5), and the scientific-context comparison is relevant though not fully developed (band 4).  Overall, the work aligns with the 5â6 band descriptors, earning a score of 5.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified (initial push variability, off-axis motion).
Suggestions for improvement (increasing trial count, averaging data) are realistic and feasible.
weaknesses: The relative impact of each weakness on the final Q-factor is not quantitatively explained.
Proposed improvements remain generic and do not address all identified limitations (e.g., force-release standardization, alignment fixtures).
No discussion of how to implement or test the suggested improvements in practice.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation names specific methodological limitations (band 3) and provides realistic improvements (band 2).  However, because the impact of weaknesses is not fully explained and the suggestions are somewhat generic, the work sits at the upper end of the 1â2 band moving into the 3â4 band, justifying a score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student frames the research question around a clearly specified physical system (vertical spring-mass with cardboard discs of stated diameters) and an explicitly defined measurable (damping factor Q).  The introduction supplies adequate theoretical background on SHM and damping, linking directly to the chosen variable, so the context is both specific and appropriate.  Minor gaps (e.g., lack of citation for the Q-factor expression) prevent a flawless score but still satisfy the descriptors for the 5â6 band.

Uncertainties are calculated, but the method (Â½ range of two trials) is simplistic and ignores instrument resolution and propagation through the Q calculation.  Only one overall Â±6.9 value is carried forward, and it is not used in graphing or analysis.  This shows some consideration with significant omissions, matching the middle of the 3â4 band.

Controlled-variable table shows awareness of relevance and sufficiency, and the student justifies mass compensation for the 20 cm disc.  Nevertheless, explanations remain qualitative; no discussion of trial number sufficiency, sampling duration or sensor resolution is provided.  This meets the âdescribedâ but not âexplainedâ level, so a low 3â4-band mark is appropriate.

The evaluation names two specific weaknesses (initial push and off-axis motion) but only states them and labels them âunavoidableâ; partial explanation of their impact is implied but not developed.  This corresponds to âdescribes specific methodological weaknessesâ, so the mid-band score is appropriate.

Apparatus, variables and a 12-step procedure are supplied; another experimenter could largely reproduce the work.  However, some important operational details are missing (e.g., initial displacement size, exact sensor settings, method for identifying successive maxima), leaving a few ambiguities that fit the middle of the 3â4 band.

Q values are correctly derived from the amplitude-ratio formula and averaged; a linear fit of Q versus diameter is produced.  While propagation of uncertainty and goodness-of-fit statistics are absent, numerical work is internally consistent and free from major arithmetic errors, fitting âprocessing carried out appropriately and accuratelyâ at the lower end of that description.

Proposed improvements are limited to âdo more than five trialsâ and mention of averaging; no concrete strategies for eliminating off-axis motion or quantifying release force are offered.  These are realistic but generic, fulfilling the descriptor for the 1â2 band, with both suggestions warranting the upper mark of that band.

Data tables, graphs and sample calculations are included and labelled, and units/uncertainties are usually indicated, so communication is generally clear.  Precision suffers from formatting errors in the raw-data table, but the presentation still satisfies the âclear and generally preciseâ criterion in the 3â4 band, meriting the top of that band.

The student relates results to published discussion of damping proportional to velocity and cites a research paper.  The comparison is relevant but brief and largely qualitative, lacking depth such as numerical agreement or discussion of Q factors in literature.  This aligns with âsome relevant comparisonâ, thus 3â4 band at the top end.

The conclusion restates the main finding and ties it directly to the quantitative trend in the processed data, showing consistency.  Although statistical strength is not discussed, the linkage between data, hypothesis and physical reasoning is clear and justified, satisfying the 5â6 band; minor analytical weaknesses warrant awarding 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: Figure 1âs axes lack labels and units; adding these is essential for clear communication of displacement, velocity, and acceleration.
Figure 2 effectively illustrates energy interchange in SHM, reinforcing the theoretical description of kinetic and potential energy.
The descriptive paragraph on damping reiterates previous content. Condense and directly link to how cardboard discs influence damping.
The Q factor formula is stated without intermediate derivation steps; include algebraic manipulations to show how it simplifies to the working expression.
The simplified expression $$Q=2\pi\frac{x_\text{max}^2}{x_f^2 - x_i^2}$$ appears without defining symbols $x_f$ and $x_i$; clarify these in the text.
The research question is specific and contextualized by detailing the springâmass system and disc diameters, aiding focus and reproducibility.
The definition of the damping factor Q lacks a citation or referenceâinclude a source for the formula to strengthen theoretical grounding.
The anecdotal narrative in the introduction is engaging but overly informal; focus on concise scientific background relevant to damping.
The formula $$T=1/f$$ is correctly stated and supports understanding of oscillatory periodâfrequency relationship.
The hypothesis misinterprets Q: a higher Q indicates less damping. Revise wording or use a damping constant that increases with damping strength.
The independent variable and its uncertainty are clearly specified, ensuring precise control over the primary manipulated factor.
The controlledâvariables table is thorough; consider also controlling ambient temperature and humidity to account for air viscosity changes.
Apparatus list misses key details: specify the motion sensorâs sampling rate and describe any calibration procedure.
The apparatus inventory is comprehensive and demonstrates careful planning of equipment selection.
Procedure omits how the initial displacement is set and how data recording is triggered; add these details to ensure reproducibility.
The displacementâtime graphs lack axis labels and units; adding these elements is vital for clear data communication.
The processed Qâfactor table presents averages with Â±6.9 uncertainty but does not show how this uncertainty affects the plotted trend; integrate error propagation.
The sample calculation of Q clearly demonstrates substitution into the formula, aiding reader comprehension of the process.
Uncertainty estimation via halfârange of two trials neglects propagation through the Q equation and instrument resolution; use full errorâpropagation methods.
The trend graph lacks error bars and statistical metrics (e.g., RÂ²), limiting assessment of linearity; include these to support the fit.
Integration of peerâreviewed research in the discussion strengthens the conclusion by situating findings within accepted scientific context.
The conclusion succinctly connects results to the hypothesis and theoretical framework, providing a coherent summary of findings.
Evaluation identifies specific weaknesses but lacks quantitative analysis of their impact; discuss how offâaxis motion or random release affects Q values.
Bibliography entries vary in format and completeness; apply a consistent citation style (e.g., APA) and include full author details and publication dates.
Raw data table contains outliers (e.g., 0.865, 0.771) that deviate from expected trends; verify these measurements and correct potential dataâentry errors.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
